name: Apple A15 Bionic
notableFeature: 5-core Neural Engine at 15.8 TOPS; powers Cinematic Mode and Live Text

name: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 1
notableFeature: Hexagon 32 NPU; first Snapdragon on Samsung 4nm GAAFET-adjacent node

name: Apple A17 Pro
notableFeature: First 3nm mobile chip (TSMC N3B); hardware ray-tracing GPU and 35 TOPS Neural Engine

name: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Gen 3
notableFeature: On-device generative AI; Hexagon NPU delivers 98 TOPS for LLM inference

name: MediaTek Dimensity 9300
notableFeature: First all-big-core flagship; APU 790 delivers 33 TOPS for on-device LLMs

name: Samsung Exynos 2400
notableFeature: NPU at 34.7 TOPS; first Exynos GPU (Xclipse 940, AMD RDNA) with hardware ray tracing

name: Apple A18 Pro
notableFeature: Second-gen 3nm (TSMC N3E); 16-core Neural Engine at 35 TOPS runs Apple Intelligence on-device

name: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite
notableFeature: First phone SoC with Qualcomm's own custom Oryon CPU (from Nuvia); TSMC N3E, 2024

name: MediaTek Dimensity 9400
notableFeature: 30 billion transistors on TSMC N3E; second-gen all-big-core with agentic on-device AI

name: Apple A19 Pro
notableFeature: TSMC N3P; matrix-multiply Neural Accelerators built into every GPU core — single-core leader (iPhone 17 Pro, 2025)

name: Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5
notableFeature: Highest mobile clock to date at 4.61 GHz; TSMC N3P with on-device agentic AI (2025)

name: MediaTek Dimensity 9500
notableFeature: Arm Lumex C1 cores on TSMC N3P; expanded NPU for on-device generative AI (2025)
